verses.text: 'But now' the tables are turned: men younger than he, whose worthless fathers he would have scorned, mock him. His honour has crashed to contempt. How swiftly the world's esteem can collapse into derision.

'But now' the tables are turned: men younger than he, whose worthless fathers he would have scorned, mock him. His honour has crashed to contempt. How swiftly the world's esteem can collapse into derision.

'But now' the tables are turned: men younger than he, whose worthless fathers he would have scorned, mock him. His honour has crashed to contempt. How swiftly the world's esteem can collapse into derision.

'But now' the tables are turned: men younger than he, whose worthless fathers he would have scorned, mock him. His honour has crashed to contempt. How swiftly the world's esteem can collapse into derision.

These mockers are themselves outcasts, driven from society, skulking among the cliffs, base and nameless men. The lowest now look down on Job. When God withdraws His hedge, even the vilest feel free to despise the fallen.

These mockers are themselves outcasts, driven from society, skulking among the cliffs, base and nameless men. The lowest now look down on Job. When God withdraws His hedge, even the vilest feel free to despise the fallen.

These mockers are themselves outcasts, driven from society, skulking among the cliffs, base and nameless men. The lowest now look down on Job. When God withdraws His hedge, even the vilest feel free to despise the fallen.

'Now am I their song,' Job laments; they jeer and spit at him, emboldened because God has loosed his cord and brought him low. His weakness invites their cruelty. Men often kick the one whom God has humbled.

'Now am I their song,' Job laments; they jeer and spit at him, emboldened because God has loosed his cord and brought him low. His weakness invites their cruelty. Men often kick the one whom God has humbled.

'Now am I their song,' Job laments; they jeer and spit at him, emboldened because God has loosed his cord and brought him low. His weakness invites their cruelty. Men often kick the one whom God has humbled.

They rise against him on every side, breaking up his path and pressing his ruin, his dignity blown away like the wind. He feels assaulted from all directions. Affliction can make a man feel besieged by the whole world.

His soul is poured out, his bones pierced through the restless nights, his disease clinging like a tight garment. Pain gives him no relief. There are sufferings of body that wear the spirit down without ceasing.

His soul is poured out, his bones pierced through the restless nights, his disease clinging like a tight garment. Pain gives him no relief. There are sufferings of body that wear the spirit down without ceasing.

His soul is poured out, his bones pierced through the restless nights, his disease clinging like a tight garment. Pain gives him no relief. There are sufferings of body that wear the spirit down without ceasing.
Cast into the mire, he cries, 'I cry unto thee, and thou dost not hear me.' Heaven seems shut against him. The hardest trial of all is to pray and feel that God is silent.
Cast into the mire, he cries, 'I cry unto thee, and thou dost not hear me.' Heaven seems shut against him. The hardest trial of all is to pray and feel that God is silent.

He feels God has turned cruel, opposing him with a strong hand, and will bring him down to death. He misreads the Father as a foe. In deep darkness even faith may falsely accuse the God it still clings to.

He feels God has turned cruel, opposing him with a strong hand, and will bring him down to death. He misreads the Father as a foe. In deep darkness even faith may falsely accuse the God it still clings to.

Did he not weep for the troubled and grieve for the poor? Yet when he looked for good, evil came, and for light, darkness. His kindness seems unrewarded. The godly are not promised exemption from sorrow in this life.

Did he not weep for the troubled and grieve for the poor? Yet when he looked for good, evil came, and for light, darkness. His kindness seems unrewarded. The godly are not promised exemption from sorrow in this life.

His inward parts churn, he goes about in gloom, and his harp and pipe are tuned now only to mourning and weeping. Joy has turned wholly to grief. Yet even this lament is poured out to God, who will not leave His servant in the dust forever.
